page: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1D cache. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in single-core and multi-core operations. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2042.0 and a multi-core score of 16898.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. It demonstrates strong memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks at 205.24 GB/sec, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.99 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 199.18 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, it achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small (135M) LLM models at 347.06 tokens/sec, and strong static web serving throughput of 55.94 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ls_v6 offers a high-density compute profile with a low memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a resource-efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) accelerates vector and matrix math without the need for dedicated GPU hardware. This server is suited for compute-intensive workloads, including web serving, software compilation, text and image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores), 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show strong performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2042.0 and multi-core score of 16898.0, alongside top-tier text generation speeds of 347.06 tokens/sec for small LLM models. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance provides a cost-effective, high-density compute profile. It is suited for CPU-bound workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and advanced AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets for high-density CPU workloads.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for CPU-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Strong multi-threaded decompression and static web serving throughput
Best suited for software compilation, image processing, and web serving
